rulings.Case Title: YES IN MY BACK YARD, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 7, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER YES IN MY BACK YARD, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT, SONJA TRAUSS Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is OFF CALENDAR. This matter is not ready for determination based on the parties' persistent supplemental filings without leave of Court well beyond moving papers, opposition and reply. While these filings have been eventually stricken pursuant to stipulation (2 court days before the continued hearing) at least one of these filings was made in support of an authorized filing (reply brief filed on September 15, 2023); moving party did not request a leave to file an amended reply removing the now potentially irrelevant parts or any declaration addressing whether the removal of the declaration in support of the reply changed the reply itself, leaving it for the Court to determine which parts of the reply are to be considered and which are not in light of the declaration being stricken. When parties are ready to present their final arguments for consideration (not an ever-changing moving target) the petition for writ of mandate may be set for hearing. The briefs on such petition will be limited to moving papers, opposition and reply. There will be no filing past the reply deadline. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL VS. NEWREZ, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 7, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT NEWREZ LLC, F/K/A NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for violation of Civil Code 2923.7 and 2923.10 is sustained without leave to amend. Notwithstanding multiple opportunities to state a currently viable cause of action for a remedy contemplated by HBOR, Plaintiff failed to do so and failed to suggest in opposition what factual allegations may be made to assert such a claim based on the posture of the foreclosure proceedings. Demurer to the cause of action for UCL violation is overruled. Plaintiff alleges loss of money stemming from the now expired foreclosure attempt. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that this "loss of money" has to be subject to restitution in order to support standing. Since the cause of action for violation of CC 2923.6 stands, there is sufficient allegation of unlawful practice in the complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL VS. NEWREZ, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 7, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT NEWREZ LLC, F/K/A NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages as Plaintiff cites no basis for such recovery under the causes of action asserted in the operative complaint. rulings.Case Title: LIGHTSPEED INVESTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. DAWN M. SOUSA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Consolidate Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 7, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF LIGHTSPEED INVESTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Consolidate Discovery is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite a single authority for the proposition that discovery may be consolidated in the cases where discovery has been closed in one action and is still open in the other. In fact, Plaintiff doesn't cite a single case where consolidation for discovery purposes only was not merely mentioned in dicta/procedural history recitation of the case. The Court notes that it does not have access to O'Connor's California Practice (2023 ed.) Civil Pretrial, Ch. 5-H 4.1; Plaintiff did not provide a copy of this secondary source and it is not clear if any cases supporting Plaintiff's position are cited therein. The court further notes, that if these matters were to be consolidated for discovery only, discovery would track action 602059 and would be closed in both cases as of October 9, 2023. This does not appear to be the outcome sought by the moving party. The parties are welcome to stipulate to the use of discovery obtained in either case to be used in the other matter in order to preserve resources. The Court will not be addressing any such stipulation on this motion as it is not properly before the Court. Late-filed opposition and sur-reply are not considered and are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROISIN LEAHY VS. ZEBRA VALLEJO, LP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 7, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT ZEBRA VALLEJO, LP, MONICA ZIMMERMAN, ALAN ZIMMERMAN MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts to support conscious disregard of tenant's rights (unabated construction noise from October 2022 to March 2023). Whether these allegations are true is not an issue to be addressed at this stage of the litigation. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
